questions.q: Find the position vector of the point which divides the line joining the points P (2, 3, -4) and Q (-5, 4, 5) in the ratio 2:3 internally.
If \vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} - \hat{k}, \vec{b} = - \hat{i} + 4\hat{j} + 5\hat{k} and \vec{c} = \hat{i} + 2\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}, find the value of [\vec{a} \vec{b} \vec{c}].
Find the unit vector in the direction of the vector \vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 6\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}.
Find the projection of the vector \vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} - 4\hat{k} on the vector \vec{b} = 3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} + 6\hat{k}.
If \vec{a} and \vec{b} are two vectors such that \vec{a} + \vec{b} = \hat{i} + 2\hat{j} + 3\hat{k} and \vec{a} - \vec{b} = 4\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + \hat{k}, find \vec{a} and \vec{b}.
If \vec{a} = \hat{i} + 2\hat{j} + 4\hat{k} and \vec{b} = 2\hat{i} - \hat{j} + \hat{k}, find the value of [\vec{a} \vec{b}].
Find the vector equation of the line passing through the point A (-1, 1, 2) and parallel to the vector \vec{b} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} + 4\hat{k}.
If \vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} + 4\hat{k} and \vec{b} = 3\hat{i} + 2\hat{j} - \hat{k}, find the value of [\vec{a} \vec{b}].
Find the position vector of the mid-point of the line joining the points A (2, 3, 4) and B (-5, 4, -3).
If \vec{a} = 2\hat{i} - \hat{j} + 2\hat{k} and \vec{b} = 3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} - 5\hat{k}, find the value of [\vec{a} \vec{b}].
